title: Add Using a Number Line
description: Add a number by making forward jumps on a number line from the starting value.
condition: Given an addition expression a + b and a number line representation.
response: Start at a and make forward jumps totaling b to find the sum.
label: Production / Skill
example: To solve $49 + 7$, start at 49, jump +1 to 50, then +6 to 56. The sum is $56$.
notes: Procedural use of number line representation.

title: Compare Numbers by Place Value
description: Compare two numbers by examining hundreds, tens, then ones digits from left to right.
condition: Given two whole numbers written in standard, word, or unit form.
response: Compare digits by place value and write >, <, or =.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Applies across representations (e.g., 6 hundreds 4 ones vs six hundred forty).

title: Convert Expanded Form to Standard Form
description: Combine place value addends to write the number in standard form.
condition: Given a number written as a sum of hundreds, tens, and ones (e.g., 90 + 800 + 1).
response: Add the place value parts to write the standard numeral.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Unit Form to Standard Form
description: Translate unit form (hundreds, tens, ones) into a standard three-digit number.
condition: Given a number in unit form such as '3 hundreds 1 ten' or '3 tens 17 ones'.
response: Compute the total value and write the standard numeral.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Includes regrouping when ones exceed 9 (e.g., 3 tens 17 ones = 47).

title: Interpret Base-Ten Model
description: Determine a number represented by base-ten blocks or place value chart dots.
condition: Given a visual model showing hundreds, tens, and ones.
response: Count each place and combine to state the total number.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Relies on visual recognition of base-ten representations.

title: Represent Number in Multiple Forms
description: Express a number in standard, word, unit, and expanded forms.
condition: Given a whole number or base-ten representation.
response: Write equivalent standard, word, unit, and expanded forms.
label: Rule / Plan
example: $310$ → word: 'three hundred ten'; unit: '3 hundreds 1 ten'; expanded: $300 + 10$.
notes: Conventional language rules for word form.

title: Subtract Using a Number Line
description: Subtract a number by making backward jumps on a number line.
condition: Given a subtraction expression a − b and a number line representation.
response: Start at a and make backward jumps totaling b to find the difference.
label: Production / Skill
example: To solve $33 - 4$, start at 33, jump back 4 to 29.
